reasoning: Donald Trump accepted an invitation to visit China during a phone call with Xi Jinping on June 5, 2025, but no specific date was set. Trade teams from the US and China only began initial talks in London on June 9, suggesting that any state visit is still in the very early planning stages. Furthermore, Trump's schedule for the remainder of June leaves virtually no room for a massive logistical undertaking like a state visit to China. He is scheduled to attend the G7 Summit in Canada from June 15-17 and the NATO Summit in the Netherlands from June 24-26. With only 15 days until the July 1 deadline and no trip announced, a visit to China in this timeframe is logistically unfeasible.
